feed.title: Kiyomizu’s Homepage
feed.subtitle: Matthew 7:13 “Enter in by the narrow gate; for the gate is wide and the way is broad that leads to destruction, and there are many who enter in by it.

entry.title: Notes of Reading (I)
entry.content:
Reading plan:

  • Alexis de Tocqueville. L’Ancien Régime et la Révolution. Bejing: The Commercial Press, 1992.
  • John Rawls, HE Huaihong, HE Baogang and LIAO Shenbai. A Theory of Justice. Beijing: China Social Sciences Pub. House, 1999.
  • GAO Xingjian. Soul Mountain. Guilin: Lijiang Pub. House, 2000.
  • GUO Shaoyu. History of Ancient Chinese Literary Criticism (Volume One). Bejing: The Commercial Press, 2010.
  • TIAN Yuqing. Politics of Powerful families in the Eastern-Jin. Beijing: Peking University Press, 2012.

Movies:

  • Doctor Zhivago, 1965.
  • Apocalypse Now, 1979.
  • Four Days of Snow and Blood, 1989.
  • Steel Rain, 2017.
  • Steel Rain 2: Summit, 2020.



Kiyomizu

October 8th, 2022

entry.title: Memories of life (I)
entry.content:
Reading and writing book notes were an integral part of my life for several years before and after high school. At that time, I liked to read “Reference News”, “Life Weekly” and “Dushu” magazines. I always went to the bookstore near the school, to the school library for the books that hadn’t been loaned out for decades. However, recently, WeChat official account articles, bilibili videos and Zhihu answers have become my main sources of information. In addition to the materials that I have to write, I seldom pick up a pen.

These words sound like nostalgic clichés: the past was slower, more enjoyable, and purer than the present. Maybe, but I don’t think so. Although some aspects of the world are on the decline, my life is generally better than it was in the past. If you give me a chance to return to the age of sixteen or seventeen, I don’t want to, because I was too lonely at that time.

In order to relieve loneliness, I read boring books, write reading notes with titles, talk about things beyond my experience from my limited life experience, and try to pretend to be stubborn old man in a young age. In a word: Beautiful but muddled So the “purity” at that time was caused by the current situation, even accidental, not an ideal state. But I still want to thank the person who has guided me to read books that have shaped my way of looking at things to a large extent, making me different and distinct from my peers in both good and bad ways.

My high school Chinese teacher is a wise educator. “Education is not about indoctrination, but lighting the torch”; “Education is not about dragging, but one cloud pushes another”, which is her portrayal. She recommended us many good books, attached gentle encouraging comments on each of our reading notes. Even now it seems that my essays are lacking in style, and dogmatic in thinking. †

Since college, I have had the idea many times: “Besides study and work, I should re-read some books I am interested in and write some reading notes”, but I have never insisted on it for more than three days. The reason is no more than: too busy, troublesome, no one is watching. The first is a hypocritical excuse, because there is obviously a lot of time to spend on electronic information flow; the second may have some truth, since college has been “running to school”, and there is often only one computer in the bag. But there is no pen and paper; the third point is real, I used to have at least one serious reader, but now I often don’t dare to show the people around me because the things I write are too hypocritical, and I don’t think I should. waste their time.

I learned a lot of knowledge and principles in college, the most important of which is to “treat real life pragmatically”. Reading has been used by me as a means of escaping the truth, but after so many days, reading should take a different attitude now. In order to maintain balance, it is good to leave some leisure gaps in addition to acquiring knowledge, training ability, and a rich resume. Instead of letting the clutter of videos and news smear these blanks, it’s better to read a little interesting book and write your own notes.

About a year ago, I first learned about two ancient blogging sites, Richard Stallman’s Personal Site and Lu Changhai’s Personal Page. They are all over two decades old and carry a large amount of text, including essays, photos, memories, opinions, and excerpts from readings. I think this is a good form, so I set up this static website and plan to update it for a long time. Maybe in the vast Internet, there will be people who come to this site by accident.

Different from the above two, the content of this site is limited to the notes of reading, life memories and a little knowledge. I will not share the current life or specific identification information here. But if you’d like to discuss or share interesting books, my email is kiyomizu.e@foxmail.com, letters are welcome.



Kiyomizu

October 7th, 2022
entry.summary: Reading and writing book notes were an integral part of my life for several years before and after high school. At that time, I liked to read “Reference News”, “Life Weekly” and “Dushu” magazines. I always went to the bookstore near the school, to the school library for the books that hadn’t been loaned out for decades. However, recently, WeChat official account articles, bilibili videos and Zhihu answers have become my main sources of information. In addition to the materials that I have to write, I seldom pick up a pen.
